top_signals.title: Oil Set for Worst Month Since 2020 as US, Iran Near Longer Truce
top_signals.summary: Oil fell as the US and Iran tentatively agreed to extend a ceasefire by 60 days, with Brent set for the biggest monthly drop since 2020 on optimism that flows through the Strait of Hormuz may resume.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Mitsui seeks LNG investments as data centers boost power demand
top_signals.summary: Mitsui & Co. is looking to invest in liquefied natural gas (LNG) projects across the Middle East, the United States and Australia, its CEO has said, as the Japanese trading house positions itself to meet rising power demand from data centers worldwide.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Decisions taken by the Governing Council of the ECB (in addition to decisions setting interest rates)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Deepinder Goyal: We have a better shot than even Amazon at winning quick commerce
top_signals.summary: Pain is inevitable but suffering optional for Zomato co-founder
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Disney says US regulator aims to ‘suppress speech’ with licence review
top_signals.summary: FCC has ordered the ABC owner to reapply for broadcast permissions after clash with Trump administration
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Japanese Banks Stoke Hybrid Bond Boom to Fund Regulatory Capital
top_signals.summary: Japan’s major banks are set for their busiest fiscal year in over a decade for issuing a special category of bonds that will finance higher capital requirements.

top_signals.title: US to designate Brazilian drug cartels as terrorist groups
top_signals.summary: Move is likely to reignite tensions between Donald Trump and Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva

top_signals.title: Data center server energy use grows across the commercial building stock
top_signals.summary: In the Annual Energy Outlook 2026 (AEO2026), our long-term outlook, we project electricity consumed by data center servers will increase across the commercial building stock, increasing more in standalone data centers than in all other data center rooms combined. By 2050, server consumption alone reaches between 446 billion kilowatthours (BkWh) and 818 billion BkWh. The highest end of the range reflects faster growth in server power draw and installed stock in our High Electricity Demand case. Standalone data centers are represented in the other buildings category, where we project servers will consume 581 BkWh of electricity in 2050 in our High Electricity Demand case. Across all cases, servers alone accounted for an estimated 7% of commercial sector electricity consumption in 2025. Data center server electricity use grows to 22%–33% of commercial building electricity use by 2050 across our cases.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
